summary: Phase 55.1 Regression Fix Testing: Verified that the 'Maximum update depth exceeded' infinite loop error is FIXED. The fix involved moving NON_INTERACTIVE_TYPES to module scope and adding Set content comparison in onHiddenFieldsChange. All guided fill-in features work correctly: Fill In badge visible (67x28 px), Previous/Next navigation works, ConfirmSubmitDialog opens from Finish.

warnings.issue: One-time 'Cannot update a component while rendering a different component' React warning on initial render. This is a development warning, not a functional bug. Does not cause infinite loops or performance issues.
warnings.recommendation: Can be addressed in future by wrapping onHiddenFieldsChange call in setTimeout(fn, 0) to defer to next tick, but not critical

critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: NON_INTERACTIVE_TYPES moved to module scope (line 78) - no longer recreated per render
FIXED: lastEmittedHiddenRef added to compare Set contents before calling onHiddenFieldsChange (lines 388-403)
navigableFields memo deps no longer include NON_INTERACTIVE_TYPES - stable reference
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 55.1 infinite loop fix verified. All Phase 55 features working: Fill In badge visible and not clipped, Previous button navigation works (hidden before Start, visible when past first navigable field), Next button advances correctly, ConfirmSubmitDialog opens from Finish. One minor React warning remains but does not affect functionality.

infinite_loop_fix.cannot_update_component_warning: PRESENT (1x on initial render) - Not a functional issue
